extension.description: Browse movies and TV shows in TMDBHelper, pick a source from Usenet, and stream it directly in Kodi. NZB-DAV searches your NZBHydra2 indexers, downloads through nzbdav, and plays the file over WebDAV — no manual NZB handling required. Includes smart quality filtering, on-the-fly MP4 to MKV remuxing with subtitle support, and full seeking with a progress bar.

Browse movies and TV shows in TMDBHelper, pick a source from Usenet, and stream it directly in Kodi. NZB-DAV searches your NZBHydra2 indexers, downloads through nzbdav, and plays the file over WebDAV — no manual NZB handling required. Includes smart quality filtering, on-the-fly MP4 to MKV remuxing with subtitle support, and full seeking with a progress bar.
extension.disclaimer: Requires your own NZBHydra2 and nzbdav instances plus valid API/WebDAV access. No content is provided.
Requires your own NZBHydra2 and nzbdav instances plus valid API/WebDAV access. No content is provided.
extension.news:
v1.0.4 - Fix the playback wedge from a slow-trickle upstream. When a Usenet article fetch took >25s (under the 30s per-read socket timeout), bytes dripped in below playable rate, Kodi's CFileCache underran, audio stalled, and subsequent seeks did NOT trigger a fresh range request — the player just sat at "Seeking" forever with no caching message. The pass-through proxy now samples bytes-per-second over a 20s rolling window and closes the response when video throughput drops below 100 KB/s, forcing Kodi's CCurlFile to reconnect. Gated to video/* content types so legitimate audio streams aren't false-killed every 20s. - Improve proxy seek performance, most visible on multi-hundred-gigabyte files where the previous implementation stalled briefly while restarting the upstream connection. - Fix Prowlarr "Test Connection" button label so the success/failure dialog correctly identifies which indexer was tested when both NZBHydra2 and Prowlarr are configured. - Hash search/proxy cache keys with sha256. Existing cache entries are regenerated transparently on next access — no user action required. - Credential redaction sweep: API keys, bearer tokens, OAuth tokens, basic-auth passwords, and session cookies in URLs / exception messages / formatted errors are now redacted before they hit the log. Closes the H2-tier of the §H.2 audit pass. - Tighten path-traversal and SSRF guards across the addon's HTTP surface (closes remaining §H.2-high audit findings + trailing scanner alerts). - TMDBHelper player JSON now writes via xbmcvfs with a verified write-result; a disk-full or permission failure on the addon-data directory is surfaced as an install-error notification instead of silently leaving a half-written file. - Service watchdog clears all three nzbdav.* window properties (active, stream_url, stream_title) on tick, so a pre-empted playback can never leave a stale stream URL or title visible to the next play. - "Playback never started" notification is now a non-modal toast (5s) rather than a blocking modal — the previous Dialog().ok() blocked the service tick thread while the user dismissed it.
v1.0.3 - Hotfix: force-remux no longer 500s on every Authorization-carrying stream. _is_safe_ffmpeg_cmd in stream_proxy.py banned CR/LF in all argv elements, but the -headers argument LEGITIMATELY contains \r\n as the HTTP header separator required by ffmpeg's HTTP demuxer — v1.0.0-pre-alpha through v1.0.2 rejected every auth'd remux command with "Refusing to start unsafe ffmpeg command" and Kodi got an immediate HTTP 500. The check now exempts the single argv position that follows -headers; CR/LF in any other position (URL, input file, etc.) is still rejected. Regression test added.
v1.0.2 - Hotfix: find_video_file no longer rejects PROPFIND hrefs whose host differs from the configured WebDAV host. nzbdav legitimately returns its INTERNAL hostname (e.g. localhost:8080) in PROPFIND responses even when the client addresses it at a different public endpoint (e.g. 192.168.1.93:3000) — v1.0.0-pre-alpha and v1.0.1 broke those setups with repeated "Completed but no video found" retries until the resolve dialog gave up. The addon now trusts only the PATH portion of cross-origin hrefs; all follow-up requests still hit the configured WebDAV host, so an attacker cannot redirect us off-server.
v1.0.1 - Retire the ffmpeg-stderr Dolby Vision probe; replace with a pure-Python RPU parser (resources/lib/dv_rpu.py) + remote container probe (resources/lib/dv_source.py) that reads real RPU data from MP4/MKV files via HTTP range requests. The new probe can tell P7 MEL from P7 FEL, distinguish profile 8 from profile 5, and report no-DV-at-all from probe-couldn't-read — information the old stderr probe could not produce. - Hybrid DV routing matrix: P7 FEL + P8 + P5 + dv_unknown route to matroska (preserves the 2026-04-15 CAMLCodec fix for P8 fmp4 hangs); P7 MEL and non-DV route to fmp4 HLS. MEL is metadata-only EL so it does not exercise the CAMLCodec dual-layer init path that tripped P8; if field testing shows MEL also hangs, tighten the gate in stream_proxy.py to match P8. - Size caps at every attacker-controlled I/O seam. _http_range has a 16 MiB default read cap; stsz first_sample_size is clamped to 16 MiB before use as a Range upper bound. A malicious moov or an RFC-violating WebDAV server can no longer OOM 32-bit Kodi. - co64 support for MP4 chunk offsets > 4 GiB. Real DV UHD remuxes > 4 GiB use co64 instead of stco; the probe now tries stco first and falls back to co64. - MKV robustness: refuse SimpleBlock lacing (bail rather than read lace-count as a NAL length), walk BlockGroup (0xA0) + Block (0xA1) wrappers too, clamp EBML child payload to parent end. - Auth header CR/LF stripping; stream_proxy wraps the DV probe call in a try/except that falls back to matroska on any probe crash rather than killing prepare_stream. - 657 unit tests pass (up from 497 in 1.0.0-pre-alpha); real-ffmpeg integration test still green.
v1.0.0-pre-alpha - Big-file force-remux is now ON by default (force_remux_threshold_mb 0 -> 20000 MB). 100 GB DV REMUXes no longer crash 32-bit Kodi's CFileCache on pass-through. - New experimental fmp4 HLS force-remux mode (Advanced -> Force remux output format). Gives full random seek across multi-hundred-gigabyte sources for non-DV-Profile-7 content. Self-healing: any ffmpeg startup failure auto-rewrites the session to the known-good piped-Matroska path BEFORE Kodi sees a broken URL. - Submit dialog stops freezing on slow nzbdav. submit_nzb runs in a background thread with the dialog pumping every 250 ms (cancel button live, message updates), AND a concurrent queue probe adopts the new nzo_id as soon as nzbdav has enqueued the job — usually 4-8 s instead of waiting for the full /api?mode=addurl reply. - Fix router handle-hang: every dispatch path (/resolve, /install_player, /clear_cache, /settings, /configure_*, /test_*, /search auto-select) now resolves the Kodi handle even on a thrown exception. Kodi could previously hang indefinitely. - Narrow the resolver's Kodi DB cleanup to the bookmark table only, with 2 s SQLite busy timeout, LIKE wildcard escaping, and a skip when video is already playing. - Probe deadline + persistent ffmpeg.log archive: every ffmpeg probe has a 20 s wall-clock timeout (no more zombie probes), and on session close the most recent 10 sessions' logs are kept in special://temp/nzbdav-hls-logs/ for post-mortem. - Settings clamping for submit_timeout: a typo'd setting (300000 instead of 300) can no longer cascade into a multi-hour blocking call. - Bump pytest dev dep to >=9.0.3 for CVE-2025-71176. Dev-only. - New PROXY.md architecture document and `just test-integration` target that runs the actual ffmpeg binary against a synthetic test MKV. - 497 unit tests + 2 integration tests pass.
v0.6.21 - Fix stale jobs in nzbdav after timeouts and cancellations. When the addon gave up on a download (timeout, user cancel, max poll iterations, Kodi shutdown), it left the job sitting in nzbdav's queue indefinitely. Re-submitting the same NZB then hit nzbdav's duplicate-rejection path and the user got a generic "check nzbdav URL and API key" dialog with no real explanation. The addon now sends a SABnzbd-compatible queue delete (3s timeout, fail-quiet) on every abort path so the next attempt is clean. History entries are deliberately left alone so failure history stays inspectable in nzbdav's web UI (audit finding L1). - Surface nzbdav's actual error message when a submit is rejected. The submit retry loop used to hide HTTP 4xx/5xx responses behind a generic "all 3 retries failed" dialog. The addon now captures nzbdav's response body (HTML-stripped, whitespace-collapsed), shows the actual server message in the dialog, and classifies retries: HTTP 408/502/503/504 still retry 3x as transient gateway issues, but HTTP 4xx and HTTP 500/501 short-circuit immediately so it's possible to tell apart "duplicate stuck job", "bad API key", and "nzbdav crashed".
v0.6.20 - Fix brief Kodi UI freeze during resolve polling when nzbdav is momentarily unreachable. The WebDAV retry delay used time.sleep() on Kodi's main thread; it now uses xbmc.Monitor().waitForAbort(), which yields to the event loop and lets Kodi shut down cleanly while a retry is pending (C4 from internal audit). - Fix silent retry loop when WebDAV credentials are wrong. When nzbdav's queue and history APIs both returned no data, the addon used to probe the WebDAV server with the movie's human-readable title as a filename, which always returned "not found" — so an auth failure never surfaced and the resolve dialog spun until the download timeout. The probe now hits the WebDAV content root, so an invalid-credentials failure shows the "Authentication failed" dialog within one poll iteration (C3 from internal audit).
v0.6.19 - Update README to reflect the v0.6.14+ proxy architecture: every container now routes through the local stream proxy (not just MP4), and the MKV pass-through path with zero-fill recovery and native seeking is documented alongside the MP4 faststart/remux branches. - Silence pylint W0201 on `self.close_connection = True` assignments in stream_proxy.py. `close_connection` is a real `BaseHTTPRequestHandler` attribute initialized by the parent class, but pylint's static analysis can't see it, so CI was failing on two inline disables' worth of false positives.
v0.6.18 - Disable force-remux for large MKVs by default (force_remux_threshold_mb now defaults to 0). Live testing confirmed the local stream proxy's pass-through path handles 12+ GB MKVs fine on 32-bit Kodi/CoreELEC builds, and pass-through gives strictly more features than force-remux: native user seeking (via the source MKV's real Cues), v0.6.15 zero-fill recovery on missing Usenet articles, and no ffmpeg CPU overhead. v0.6.16's force-remux branch was defending against `Open - Unhandled exception` that most likely came from the PROPFIND cascade v0.6.14 already fixed, not Content-Length overflow. Users hitting real regressions on their platform can restore the previous behavior by setting force_remux_threshold_mb to 4096 (or any non-zero MB threshold). - Fix MemoryError in the pass-through proxy's upstream read loop on 32-bit Kodi. Kodi's CFileCache alone can reserve ~1.5 GB (cachemembuffersize * readbufferfactor), leaving little headroom in Python's 32-bit heap. A 1 MB upstream read chunk was hitting allocation failure when a second handler thread opened during Kodi's CCurlFile reconnect-on-error recovery. Read chunk is now 64 KB (16x smaller, allocation-friendly on a fragmented heap) and MemoryError is explicitly caught. - Force `Connection: close` on pass-through responses so the previous handler thread unwinds as soon as Kodi reconnects, instead of lingering with multi-megabyte TCP buffers and a live upstream HTTP response. - Skip the _clear_kodi_playback_state() DB cleanup when a video is already playing. Kodi's internal library cleanup thread periodically vacuums MyVideos131.db and Textures13.db on its own schedule; if our bookmark-cleanup DELETE happens to fire concurrently, the added DB contention can freeze playback. The bookmark cleanup only matters for new plays anyway, so the isPlayingVideo() guard is a zero-cost skip.
v0.6.17 - Fix zombie ffmpeg remux after a playback stall (e.g. when Kodi's automatic DB vacuum freezes the decoder). Kodi would stop consuming bytes without firing onPlayBackStopped, so ffmpeg kept writing into a dead TCP socket forever and the next replay hung with "Playback never started after 70s" - Tear down any prior stream session on every new prepare_stream, and add a 60s write timeout to the remux socket so stuck writes always unwind and kill ffmpeg - Hook NzbdavPlayer.onPlayBackStopped / onPlayBackEnded to clear active proxy sessions immediately on clean stops
v0.6.16 - Fix "Open - Unhandled exception" on >4 GB MKV files on 32-bit Kodi builds (Amlogic CoreELEC and similar) by force-remuxing large non-MP4 files through ffmpeg — Kodi never sees a Content-Length that overflows its cache/file offsets - New setting "Force ffmpeg remux above (MB)" (default 4096, 0 disables) so 64-bit Kodi users can turn it off - Copy subtitles verbatim when remuxing MKV sources (PGS/DVD/HDMV bitmap subs can't be re-encoded to SRT and were aborting the remux)
v0.6.15 - Stream proxy survives mid-playback upstream failures (missing usenet articles, brief nzbdav restarts) by probing forward to find a readable offset, zero-filling the gap, and resuming the stream — no more black screen / "Playback failed" dialog - Recovery uses retry-with-backoff (up to 30 s) so transient upstream unavailability gets a chance to come back before the region is declared unrecoverable
v0.6.14 - Fix MKV playback failing with "Playback failed to start" / "Unhandled exception" - Route all file types through the local stream proxy so Kodi never talks to the WebDAV server directly, avoiding a PROPFIND parent-directory scan that cascaded into an Open failure - Forward tmdb_id in the TMDBHelper play_movie template so the replay-bookmark cleanup actually matches movie entries
v0.6.13 - Make NZB submit timeout configurable (Advanced → Polling, default 30s) - Avoids hardcoded 15s timeout failing on large NZBs that nzbdav takes longer to parse
v0.6.12 - Fix replay of the same NZB failing with "Playback failed to start" - Clear Kodi's stale bookmarks/settings/streamdetails before each play so auto-resume doesn't misfire on plugin URLs
v0.6.11 - Preserve full modal error dialog text where Kodi's Dialog API allows it - Show NZBHydra outages and invalid responses in modal dialogs - Keep duplicate NZB results visible instead of merging indexers
v0.6.10 - Show modal error dialog on every playback failure (replaces toast notifications) - Detect post-resolve stream crashes (missing articles, auth rejection) with error dialog - Clear playlist on stream failure to prevent TMDBHelper retry loop
v0.6.9 - Deduplicate identical NZBs from multiple indexers (same title+size), show "Multiple" as indexer - Send configured max results limit to NZBHydra2 API (was hardcoded to 100)
v0.6.8 - Fix playback failing due to URL-encoded '=' in auth header (Kodi pipe syntax) - Show download failures in a modal dialog instead of a toast notification - Clear video playlist on resolve failure to prevent Kodi retry loop
v0.6.7 - Fix pylint W0621: remove redundant import os in _serve_temp_faststart
v0.6.6 - Validate URL scheme in stream proxy to reject non-HTTP URLs - Redact API keys and auth credentials from debug logs - Reuse shared notification helper (remove duplicated logic) - Reduce _serve_remux complexity by extracting seek and faststart helpers - Localize "no video file found" notification string - Truncate server failure messages to 80 chars for TV UI - Fix all code scanning alerts (complexity, indentation, broad-except)
v0.6.5 - Show nzbdav failure reason to user when download fails - Notify user when download completes but no video file found on WebDAV - Show stream errors to user instead of silently swallowing them - Fix playback freeze caused by ffmpeg stderr pipe buffer filling up
v0.6.2 - Fix flaky cache eviction test due to non-deterministic file sizes
v0.6.1 - Fix remaining code scanning alerts (broad-except, locally-disabled)
v0.6.0 - Fix all code scanning alerts (complexity, broad-except, continuation) - Fix pylint warnings (nesting, unused import, consider-using-in)
v0.5.0 - Simplify settings: TMDBHelper-only install, remove WebDAV URL field - Release group multiselect dialogs with curated defaults - Fix continuation requests restarting ffmpeg from byte 0
v0.4.3 - Remux MP4 to MKV on the fly via ffmpeg (fixes 32-bit Kodi CFileCache bug) - Subtitle conversion (mov_text to SRT) with toggleable setting - Byte-range seeking with duration probe and progress bar - Background service for stream proxy and playback auto-recovery - Fix playback-failed dialog caused by setResolvedUrl
v0.4.1 - Address high/medium findings from code review
v0.4.0 - Show lightning bolt indicator for already-downloaded NZBs - Suppress BrokenPipeError log spam when stopping playback
v0.3.0 - WebDAV streaming on CoreELEC with MP4 faststart proxy - Skip re-downloading if NZB already completed in nzbdav history - Test Connection buttons for NZBHydra and nzbdav in settings - Retry NZB submission up to 3 times on transient failure - Show filter count and offer to show all when everything filtered - LRU cache eviction when cache exceeds 50MB limit - Use IMDB ID for TV show searches
v0.2.0 - Kodi localization support for addon UI and settings labels - Repository best-practice metadata and coverage tests
v0.1.0 - Initial release with NZBHydra2 search, nzbdav streaming, TMDBHelper integration - PTT-based quality filtering, custom results dialog, search caching
